explanation: The property states that globally, whenever the robot is engaged in path planning activities (pathPlanning), it should explain the path it is planning to take (explainPath) and justify the decision (justifyDecision). This directly translates the request to explain the path and justify its optimality whenever path planning occurs.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the robot is engaged in path planning activities (represented by 'pathPlanning'), it must explain the path it is planning to take ('explainPath') and justify its decision ('justifyDecision'). This ensures that the explanation and justification are always provided as long as the path planning activity is ongoing.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the robot is currently engaged in path planning activities (pathPlanning), then it must simultaneously (at the same time step) explain the path it is planning to take (explainPath) AND justify its decision (justifyDecision). This captures the conditional requirement for the robot to provide an explanation and justification whenever it is performing path planning.

The given prompt states that the robot should "Explain path robot is planning to take and why it is the optimal choice". This implies a conditional relationship: whenever the robot is engaged in path planning, it should perform two actions: explain the path and justify the decision. Template 10, 'G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)', captures this 'always if' relationship. Here, 'bool_exp2' represents the precondition 'pathPlanning', and 'bool_exp3' represents the conjunction of the two required actions: 'explainPath & justifyDecision'. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this condition holds at all times.

The LTL formula states that globally, whenever the robot is engaged in path planning activities (bool_exp1), it must explain the path it is planning to take AND justify its decision (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the explanation and justification are conditional upon the robot performing path planning.

The property states that globally, if the robot is engaged in path planning activities (pathPlanning), then eventually it will explain the path it is planning to take and justify the decision (explainPath AND justifyDecision). This captures the requirement that the explanation and justification should follow the planning activity.

The LTL property states that globally, if the robot is engaged in path planning (pathPlanning), then it must explain the path it is planning to take (explainPath) AND justify the decision (justifyDecision). This captures the requirement for the robot to continuously explain and justify its path whenever it is in the path planning phase.

The property states that globally, if the robot is currently engaged in path planning activities (bool_exp2), then it must eventually explain the path it is planning to take and justify its decision (bool_exp3). This captures the conditional aspect of the request, meaning the explanation and justification are only required when the robot is actively planning a path.

The property states that globally (G), if the robot is currently engaged in path planning (bool_exp1), then it must simultaneously explain the path it is planning to take AND justify its decision (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the explanation and justification are directly tied to the activity of path planning.

The natural language expresses a requirement for the robot to explain its planned path and justify why it is optimal. The phrase "robot is planning to take" indicates that this requirement is contingent on the robot being engaged in path planning activities. Therefore, we use 'pathPlanning' as the antecedent of an implication. The desired actions, 'explainPath' and 'justifyDecision', are combined with a logical AND, forming the consequent. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this property holds true at all times.
